title: Best Real Estate Lead Generation Tools for 2026
description: Real Geeks, Follow Up Boss, BombBomb, and Zillow Premier Agent compared—pricing, lead guarantees, and what actually converts.
tldr: Real Geeks bundles IDX, CRM, and lead ads with guarantees starting at Expand tier. Follow Up Boss excels at CRM-first routing for teams already running paid campaigns. BombBomb delivers async video messaging for warm follow-up, not cold lead gen. Zillow Premier Agent remains the highest-traffic referral source but pricing is opaque—contact the vendor directly.

body.text: Lead generation tools split into three camps: platforms that run ads and deliver leads (Real Geeks, Zillow Premier Agent), CRMs that organize inbound leads from any source (Follow Up Boss), and engagement layers that warm leads after capture (BombBomb). Most agents need a combination. We evaluated four widely deployed options based on pricing transparency, feature depth, and how they fit solo agents versus teams.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, feature matrices, and public plan details as of May 2026. No fabricated test calls or transaction counts. We flagged which tools generate leads versus manage them, noted contractual lead guarantees where published, and surfaced hidden costs like per-user fees or add-on calling. If a vendor withholds pricing (Zillow Premier Agent), we say so.

Real Geeks packages IDX website, AI-powered SEO Fast Track, CRM, and Facebook/Instagram ad management into four tiers: Establish, Grow, Expand, and Conquer. Lead guarantees apply only to Expand and Conquer plans and are market-tier dependent—4-8 ad markets on Expand, 15-20 on Conquer. The guarantee covers the full contract term (6 or 12 months) with credits if minimums aren't met, excluding leads with invalid phone and email. Establish includes 2 CRM users, Grow 3, Expand 5, Conquer 15; additional users cost $25/month each. Optional add-ons: Geek AI assistant ($200/month), Brand Boost retargeting ($300/month on Expand/Conquer), per-listing Facebook/IG boost ads ($100 per listing), and additional MLS feeds ($100/month Establish, $50 Grow, $25 Expand/Conquer). Dedicated success manager ships with Expand and Conquer only. All plans include automated email/SMS drip workflows, CRM mobile app, 50+ integrations, and 24/7 phone support.

body.name: Real Geeks
body.price: Request pricing; Expand and Conquer tiers include lead guarantees
pros: Lead guarantee on Expand/Conquer plans with published minimum ranges
AI-powered SEO Fast Track and custom-branded property search app included
Facebook ad tool with full-service account management on higher tiers
CRM, IDX website, and ad delivery in one stack
cons: Establish and Grow tiers exclude lead guarantees and dedicated success manager
Add-on costs (Geek AI, Brand Boost, extra MLS feeds) stack quickly
Lead guarantee invalidated if you modify templated website code or change target markets after 30 days

body.text: Follow Up Boss is a CRM, not a lead source. It ingests leads from any origin (Zillow, Facebook, referrals, open houses), routes them via customizable Smart Lists, and automates Action Plans. Three plans: Grow at $69/month per user (monthly) or $58/month (annual), Pro at $499/month for 10 users ($416 annual) with unlimited calling/texting and team inboxes, Platform at $1,000/month for 30 users ($833 annual) with teams-within-teams and dedicated success manager. Grow plan requires a $39/user add-on for calling ($33 annual); Pro and Platform include unlimited calling/texting. Additional users cost $49/month on Pro, $20 on Platform. All tiers include AI features (smart summaries, suggested tasks, predictive lead prioritization), though Grow's AI works best with the calling add-on. No contracts, 7-day-a-week phone/email support, free trial.

body.name: Follow Up Boss
body.price: $69/month Grow (add calling $39/user), $499/month Pro (10 users), $1,000/month Platform (30 users)
pros: Connects any lead source and website; vendor-agnostic architecture
Unlimited contacts, lead sources, and integrations on all plans
AI smart summaries and predictive prioritization included across tiers
cons: Does not generate leads—only organizes and nurtures them
Calling costs extra on Grow plan ($39/user monthly, $33 annual)
AI features on Grow underperform without calling add-on data
Per-user pricing scales fast for larger teams

body.text: BombBomb layers asynchronous video messaging into email and CRM workflows. It does not generate leads. Three plans: Core at $42/month per user ($36 annual) with unlimited video messages, mobile apps, browser extensions (Chrome, Edge), email integrations (Gmail, Outlook), quick editing, custom branding, and SOC 2 Type II hosting. Core + Copilot at $70/month ($56 annual) adds AI speaker notes, summaries, titles, noise reduction, smart team assignments, fallback video logic, CRM integrations (Microsoft Dynamics, Outreach, Total Expert), and automated workflows. Enterprise (custom pricing) adds SSO (SCIM/Okta), API access, role-based access controls, advanced admin governance, dedicated success manager, and enterprise onboarding. Core + Copilot aims for 100% team video adoption via Copilot's suggested scripts and example videos.

body.name: BombBomb
body.price: $42/month Core, $70/month Core + Copilot (both per user; annual discounts available)
pros: Unlimited video messages on all plans, no per-send caps
AI speaker notes, summaries, and audio enhancements on Copilot tier
White-labeled player experience with custom branding, CTAs, and clickable buttons
Integrates with Gmail, Outlook, Microsoft Dynamics, Outreach, Total Expert
cons: Not a lead generation tool—requires existing pipeline
Copilot's AI features and CRM integrations locked to $70/month tier
Per-user pricing; no team tiers like Follow Up Boss
Video engagement analytics are basic on Core plan

body.text: Zillow Premier Agent places your profile and contact info atop Zillow listings in your ZIP codes, delivering buyer and seller inquiries generated by Zillow's organic traffic (the highest in U.S. real estate). Pricing is market-dependent and not published; agents request quotes based on ZIP codes and competition. Lead volume and exclusivity vary by spend level. No public feature matrix or integration list exists. Contact Zillow directly for current pricing and lead guarantees.

body.name: Zillow Premier Agent
pros: Highest organic traffic source in U.S. real estate
Delivers buyer and seller inquiries from active home searchers
cons: No published pricing or feature details
Lead quality and exclusivity vary by ZIP and spend level
Requires separate CRM for follow-up and nurture
High-competition ZIPs command premium, opaque costs

items: Solo agents entering a market: Real Geeks Establish or Grow for bundled IDX, CRM, and Facebook ads without lead guarantees. Add Follow Up Boss Grow if you already have multiple lead sources and need better routing.

Teams closing 30+ transactions/year: Real Geeks Expand or Conquer for guaranteed lead delivery, dedicated success manager, and full-service ad management. Pair with Follow Up Boss Pro ($499/month for 10 users) for team inboxes and call reporting if leads exceed one source.

Agents with warm databases but weak follow-up: BombBomb Core + Copilot ($70/month) for AI-assisted video nurture. Integrate with existing CRM (Follow Up Boss, Outreach, Total Expert) to automate video drops in drip campaigns.

High-budget agents in competitive ZIPs: Zillow Premier Agent for top-of-funnel volume from active searchers. Requires Follow Up Boss or Real Geeks CRM for lead management since Zillow does not provide CRM tooling.
Real Geeks Establish for teams—only 2 CRM users included, no lead guarantee, no dedicated success manager. Expand or Conquer make more sense once you're closing volume.
Follow Up Boss Grow without the calling add-on if your leads are phone-first. AI features underperform without call data, and $39/month per user for calling approaches Pro pricing fast.
BombBomb for cold prospecting. Video works for warm follow-up, past clients, and sphere; it does not replace a lead generation engine.
Zillow Premier Agent in low-traffic rural ZIPs where cost-per-lead may exceed Facebook or Google PPC efficiency. Run the math with Zillow before committing.
